card.intuition: Classify points as core (≥ minPts neighbours within ε), border (within ε of a core point), or noise. Clusters are maximal sets of density-connected core points. DBSCAN finds arbitrary-shaped clusters and automatically identifies outliers.
card.analogy: A population map: dense cities (core points) expand to include nearby suburbs (border points); isolated farmhouses (noise) don't belong to any city. Cities are defined by density, not shape — an oddly shaped city is still a city.
card.time_complexity: O(n log n) with spatial indexing; O(n²) brute force
when_to_use: Clusters with non-spherical shapes (crescent, ring, blobs)
Automatic number of clusters (no k to specify)
Anomaly detection: noise points are outliers
gotchas: ε and minPts are sensitive hyperparameters — use k-distance plot to set ε
DBSCAN struggles with clusters of varying density (use HDBSCAN instead)
High-dimensional data: ε meaning changes — distances become less meaningful

exercise.starter_code:
def dbscan(X, eps, min_pts):
    """
    Returns labels: -1 for noise, 0..k for cluster ids.
    """
    n      = len(X)
    labels = [-1] * n
    cluster_id = 0

    def dist(a, b):
        return sum((ai-bi)**2 for ai,bi in zip(a,b))**0.5

    def region_query(p):
        return [q for q in range(n) if dist(X[p], X[q]) <= eps]

    def expand(p, neighbours):
        labels[p] = cluster_id
        i = 0
        while i < len(neighbours):
            q = neighbours[i]
            if labels[q] == -1:
                labels[q] = cluster_id   # border point
            if labels[q] == 0:           # unvisited
                labels[q] = cluster_id
                q_nb = region_query(q)
                if ___________:          # q is a core point
                    neighbours += q_nb
            i += 1

    visited = [False] * n
    for p in range(n):
        if visited[p]: continue
        visited[p] = True
        nb = region_query(p)
        if len(nb) < min_pts:
            labels[p] = -1   # noise (tentative)
        else:
            expand(p, nb)
            cluster_id += 1
    return labels
hints: q is a core point if it has at least min_pts neighbours within eps — check len(q_nb) >= min_pts before expanding its neighbourhood into the current cluster
exercise.test_code:
# Two tight clusters + noise
cluster1 = [[1,1],[1.1,1],[1,1.1],[0.9,1]]
cluster2 = [[5,5],[5.1,5],[5,5.1],[4.9,5]]
noise    = [[10,10]]
X = cluster1 + cluster2 + noise
labels = dbscan(X, eps=0.5, min_pts=2)
# First 4 points same cluster, next 4 another, last is noise
assert len(set(labels[:4])) == 1,       'Cluster 1 should be one cluster'
assert len(set(labels[4:8])) == 1,      'Cluster 2 should be one cluster'
assert labels[:4][0] != labels[4:8][0], 'Two different clusters'
assert labels[8] == -1,                 'Noise point'
print('PASS')
